_model.note: Single source of truth for the patch library. Each entry's 'patch' is our engine's JSON (see PATCH-FORMAT.md); build_patches.py validates it, writes patches/bank/<id>.json (usable files), renders a preview mp3, and builds patches.html. Evokes = the sound it nods to (synthesized from scratch, no presets/samples).
_model.ranges: sub <80Hz · bass 80-250 · low-mid 250-550 · high-mid 550-2k · presence 2k-6k · air >6k

rules.h: 1 · Give every voice its own frequency lane
rules.body: Masking happens when two sounds fight for the same band. Assign each a home: sub <80Hz (one voice only), bass 80-250, low-mid 250-550, high-mid 550-2k (leads/keys), presence 2-6k (bells/air), air >6k. High-pass everything that isn't the bass below ~50Hz so the low end stays clean.

rules.h: 2 · One bass at a time, kept mono/centre
rules.body: Two bass sounds = mud. Pick one (sub OR saw-bass), pan it centre, and let it own the low end. If you want weight + grit, layer a sub (sine) under a saw-bass but split their ranges (sub keeps <120Hz, saw-bass takes the upper harmonics).

rules.h: 3 · Separate same-range layers by pan & timbre
rules.body: If two voices share a band (e.g. pad + strings), pan them slightly L/R and make their attacks differ — a slow swelling pad under a fast plucked arp reads as two things, not a blur. Complementary attack > identical attack.

rules.h: 4 · The scale is your comb of allowed pitches
rules.body: Pick a key + mode for the mood, then you can only land on 'right' notes: minor (dark), phrygian (ancient/dark, lowered 2nd), dorian (cool minor), lydian (soaring, raised 4th), wholetone (floating, no home). In the engine, scale(root, mode) gives the comb; degree(i) never picks a wrong note.

rules.h: 5 · Build chords from scale degrees; know consonance vs tension
rules.body: Stack every other scale note for a triad (I, IV, V = bright/stable; ii, iii, vi = softer/minor). Consonant intervals — 3rds, 5ths, 6ths, octaves — fuse and rest. Tension intervals — 2nds, 7ths, the tritone — push and want to resolve. Use tension on purpose, then release it.

rules.h: 6 · Voice-leading: move by the smallest step
rules.body: When chords change, move each voice to the nearest note of the next chord (keep common tones, step the rest). Small motion = smooth and 'musical'; big leaps in inner voices = lumpy. This is why pads glide and basses walk.

PAD (low-mid bed) + LEAD (high-mid melody) + BASS (low root) + ARP/PLUCK (mid motion) + a sprinkle of BELL/FX (air) = a full, non-masking arrangement. Avoid: two leads in the same octave (they clash), two basses (mud), three wide pads (smear). One of each role, each in its lane.

rules.h: 8 · Arrange over time, don't play it all at once
rules.body: Density is a dial. Intro = bass + pad. Build = add arp, then lead. Peak = everything + bell/FX. Outro = strip back to the bed. Use automation lanes (synth.curve) to open filters and swell levels across the piece — the arc is the composition.

patches.name: Oxygène Lead
patches.desc: A singing saw lead with gentle vibrato, tape-style delay and reverb — the Oxygène melody voice.
patches.recipe: saw → resonant LPF (open, resonant) → pitch-LFO vibrato → ping-pong delay + hall.
patches.pairs_with: over a pad + bass; answer it with an arp

patches.name: Pulse Lead
patches.desc: A hollow square lead with a slow filter sweep — bright, vocal, retro.
patches.recipe: square → LPF with slow filter-LFO → reverb.
patches.pairs_with: cuts through a dense pad; keep one lead only

patches.name: Glass Lead
patches.desc: A cold, glassy FM lead that dulls as it sustains — crystalline and futuristic.
patches.recipe: 2-op FM (ratio 3, moderate index) → LPF → wide reverb.

patches.name: CS-80 Brass
patches.desc: Three detuned saws through a 12dB lowpass with an envelope swell, drenched in hall — the Blade Runner brass.
patches.recipe: 3× saw (detuned) → LPF with strong filter-envelope (the 'aftertouch' swell) → drive → huge reverb, wide.
patches.pairs_with: as the emotional bed; one held chord says everything

patches.name: Phased Strings
patches.desc: A lush 5-voice string machine, very wide — the phased-string bed of Équinoxe/Oxygène.
patches.recipe: 5× detuned saw → soft LPF → maximum stereo width + hall (phaser approximated by detune+width).
patches.pairs_with: under a lead; don't stack two wide pads

patches.name: Warm Pad
patches.desc: A soft additive pad with a slow swell — the gentle bed everything rests on.
patches.recipe: additive (falling harmonics) → soft LPF → slow attack → hall.
patches.pairs_with: the universal bed; pair with any lead

patches.name: Drawbar Organ
patches.desc: Additive sine harmonics = the drawbars of a tonewheel organ. Bright, vocal, immediate.
patches.recipe: additive (drawbar registration) → open LPF → instant attack → light reverb.
patches.pairs_with: a lead voice or on its own; add a sub-bass under it

patches.name: Cathedral Organ
patches.desc: A big additive pipe-organ with many ranks, in a vast reverb — solemn and wide.
patches.recipe: additive (rich harmonics) → LPF → gentle attack → cathedral hall, wide.
patches.pairs_with: stands alone; or under sparse bells

patches.name: Sub Bass
patches.desc: A near-pure low sine — felt more than heard. Owns the sub band.
patches.recipe: additive (fundamental + faint 2nd) → low LPF → centre, narrow.
patches.pairs_with: under everything; only ONE bass at a time

patches.name: Saw Bass
patches.desc: A punchy saw bass with a filter-envelope pluck — drives a track.
patches.recipe: saw → resonant LPF with filter-env → fast envelope → tight reverb.
patches.pairs_with: the driving low end; pick this OR the sub

patches.name: FM Bell
patches.desc: An inharmonic FM bell with a long shimmering tail — the classic glassy chime.
patches.recipe: 2-op FM (inharmonic ratio 3.5, high index) → long decay → big reverb.
patches.pairs_with: sparse accents over pads; the 'air' sparkle

patches.name: Stardust
patches.desc: Sparse decaying sine twinkles scattered in the field — analog cosmic shimmer.
patches.pairs_with: atmosphere over anything; keep it faint

patches.name: Sonar Ping
patches.desc: A struck pitch-drop sounding with a long ringing tail — a probe into the dark.
patches.recipe: ping oscillator (pitch-drop + body) → LPF → long decay → big reverb.

patches.name: Bubbling Arp
patches.desc: A hypnotic 16th-note arpeggio in A minor — the bubbling motion under a Jarre track.
patches.recipe: square → resonant LPF → fast pluck envelope → delay, driven by a step sequence.
patches.pairs_with: motion under a pad+lead; its own rhythmic layer
